Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists StMYB200 as DM8C10G21200.

Applied_Species.reason: The study is on potato and specifically uses potato clone 01–58 for functional analysis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper title and text identify potato as Solanum tuberosum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Functional analyses and transformations were performed in diploid clone 01–58.

Source_Species.reason: StMYB200 coding sequence/promoter materials were obtained from potato clone 01–58.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source material is potato, i.e. Solanum tuberosum.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Full-length coding sequences were amplified from tuber flesh cDNA of diploid clone 01–58.

Regulator_Type.reason: Results describe StMYB200 as one of two tandem R2R3 MYB transcription factor genes.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is explicitly one of the R2R3 MYB TF genes at the Pf locus.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The gene is directly linked in Results to anthocyanin accumulation/biosynthesis in tuber flesh.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Anthocyanin is a flavonoid; the paper discusses flavonoid and anthocyanin pathways together.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary states anthocyanin accumulation determines tuber flesh colour and influences nutritional quality.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results repeatedly specify regulation of anthocyanin accumulation in tuber flesh.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: RNA-seq and promoter assays support that StMYB200 activates anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Results show transcriptional activation of StMYB210/StbHLH1 and interaction with bHLH factor StbHLH1.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Results directly support effects on StMYB210, StbHLH1, and activation of StDFR and StF3H promoters in dual-LUC assays.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: All listed effects are shown in Results: activation of Pf-haplotype StMYB210 promoter, activation of StbHLH1 promoter, and stronger StDFR/StF3H promoter activation with StbHLH1.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Association with the Pf locus and dependence on the Pf-haplotype 1.7-kb insertion for activating StMYB210 are supported, but calling these 'upstream signals or inputs' for StMYB200 itself is interpretive rather than directly demonstrated.

Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation.reason: Results support StMYB200 activating StMYB210 and StbHLH1 within the same module; describing this as feedforward regulation is a reasonable inference from the demonstrated network.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: BiFC YFP signal was detected with nuclear marker H2B-mCherry, but the paper does not explicitly state sole subcellular localization of StMYB200 as 'Nucleus'.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Y2H, pull-down, LCI and BiFC assays support interactions with StbHLH1 and StMYB210.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Results show StMYB200 interacts with StbHLH1 and with StMYB210; the paper concludes these interactions regulate anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Figure 1 and Figure S1 show high expression in red flesh, rare/low in white flesh, and higher expression in Pf/Pf than pf/pf.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods describe 35S-driven effectors and assays using native StMYB210, StbHLH1, StDFR and StF3H promoters.

Omics_Data.reason: GWAS and RNA-seq are central to the study; data availability gives BioProject PRJNA1160339.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for StMYB200 in the Results and Methods.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: CRISPR knockout altered tuber flesh colour and reduced expression of anthocyanin pathway genes.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Results support StMYB200 as an upstream MYB at Pf that directly induces StMYB210 from the insertion-containing promoter and cooperates with StbHLH1 to drive anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each enumerated point is supported by the Results, figures, and supplementary figures.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: General breeding value for pigmented potato is supported by Summary/Discussion, but the specific claim about being 'most effective when deployed with the Pf-associated StMYB210 promoter insertion and compatible bHLH partner activity' is an extrapolation not directly tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: RT-qPCR showed StMYB200 knockout did not reduce its own transcript but did reduce StMYB210; Discussion mentions StMYB200 was also reported as StMYBA1/Stan3.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists StMYB210 as DM8C10G21210.

Applied_Species.reason: The functional analyses were carried out in potato.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Potato is identified as Solanum tuberosum in the paper.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: CRISPR and promoter analyses used the diploid clone 01–58.

Source_Species.reason: StMYB210 sequences and expression assays derive from potato materials.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Full-length coding sequence was amplified from tuber flesh cDNA of diploid clone 01–58.

Regulator_Type.reason: StMYB210 is explicitly described as an R2R3 MYB transcription factor gene.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is one of the R2R3 MYB TF genes at the Pf locus.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly link StMYB210 to anthocyanin accumulation in tuber flesh.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Anthocyanin is a flavonoid; the paper discusses flavonoid/anthocyanin pathway structural genes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary states anthocyanin accumulation determines flesh colour and influences nutritional quality.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The tested trait is anthocyanin accumulation in tuber flesh.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Knockout and promoter assays support control of anthocyanin biosynthesis gene activation.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Results show transcriptional activation, self-regulation, and protein interaction with StbHLH1.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Results support regulation of its own promoter, StbHLH1 promoter, and activation of StDFR and StF3H promoters.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Each listed effect is shown in Results: autoregulation via MBS1/MBS2 in the Pf promoter insertion, StbHLH1 promoter activation, and enhanced StDFR/StF3H activation with StbHLH1.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Results directly show activation by StMYB200 and dependence on the Pf-haplotype 1.7-kb promoter insertion for strong expression.

Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation.reason: Positive autoregulation is directly shown, and inclusion in a feedforward circuit with StMYB200/StbHLH1 is supported by the demonstrated regulatory interactions.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: BiFC signal colocalized with nuclear marker in interaction assays, but explicit independent localization of StMYB210 as nuclear is not directly stated.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Y2H, pull-down, LCI and BiFC support interaction with StbHLH1 and StMYB200.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Results show regulatory interactions with StbHLH1 and interaction with StMYB200 in anthocyanin regulation.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: High in red flesh, low/absent in white flesh, higher in Pf/Pf than pf/pf, and associated with the 1.7-kb insertion across accessions are all supported.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods describe 35S effector constructs and native StMYB210, StbHLH1, StDFR and StF3H promoter reporter assays.

Omics_Data.reason: GWAS and RNA-seq are used; RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA1160339 is given.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were applied to StMYB210 in the study.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: StMYB210 knockout altered/whitened tuber flesh colour and reduced anthocyanin pathway gene expression.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The Results directly support that the Pf-haplotype promoter insertion enables high StMYB210 expression associated with anthocyanin accumulation in tuber flesh.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All five summary points are supported by Results and supplementary data.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports breeding relevance and association of the insertion with colourful flesh, but specific claims about marker-assisted selection and cis-regulatory engineering are forward-looking applications not directly tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion notes previous names StAN1/Stan2, and Results/Discussion state expression alone may be insufficient for flesh pigmentation because structural genes/other regulators are also required.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists StbHLH1 as DM8C09G19810.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests were performed in potato clone 01–58.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Potato is Solanum tuberosum in the paper.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Knockout analysis used the red-fleshed diploid clone 01–58.

Source_Species.reason: StbHLH1 coding sequence was amplified from potato cDNA.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state full-length coding sequence of StbHLH1 was amplified from tuber flesh cDNA of diploid clone 01–58.

Regulator_Type.reason: Results explicitly describe StbHLH1 as a bHLH transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is specifically a basic helix–loop–helix transcription factor gene.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Knockout and interaction assays directly link StbHLH1 to anthocyanin accumulation in tuber flesh.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Anthocyanin is a flavonoid and the paper addresses the anthocyanin/flavonoid pathway.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Anthocyanin accumulation is stated to determine pigmented tuber flesh colour and influence nutritional quality.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results specifically concern tuber flesh anthocyanin accumulation.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Data support StbHLH1 as a MYB cofactor required for activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Results show transcriptional co-activation via interaction with MYB factors rather than strong activation alone.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: StDFR and StF3H are supported by dual-LUC; effects on its own expression and on StMYB200 expression after knockout are supported by RT-qPCR.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: All listed effects are directly described in Results and supplemental figure data.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Dual-LUC assays showed the StbHLH1 promoter is activated by StMYB200 or StMYB210.

Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation.reason: Knockout affects its own expression and increases StMYB200 expression, supporting regulatory feedback within the module.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: BiFC interaction signal overlaps with a nuclear marker, but the paper does not explicitly present a standalone localization assay assigning StbHLH1 to the nucleus.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Y2H, pull-down, LCI and BiFC all support interaction with StMYB200 and StMYB210.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper concludes StbHLH1 forms MYB–bHLH regulatory interactions with StMYB200 and StMYB210.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: RNA-seq and supplementary data show absence in StMYB200ko1/StMYB210ko1 and barely expression in pf/pf lines.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods and Results use 35S effector constructs and native StbHLH1, StDFR and StF3H promoters.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq is used and linked to BioProject PRJNA1160339.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for StbHLH1 except no GWAS claim was made here.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: CRISPR knockout caused white-flesh tubers and lowered StDFR expression.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Results support StbHLH1 as an essential co-regulator activated by both MYBs and required for strong anthocyanin gene activation in tuber flesh.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All five listed points are supported by Results and supplemental figures.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable application inference, but the paper does not directly test engineering/stacking strategies using StbHLH1.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show StbHLH1 alone did not activate StDFR promoter significantly and that knockout increased StMYB200 without changing StMYB210.
